description: Demo of the SRD light & vision pass (US-127): a sunlit verge that funnels into a pitch-black cave. Verify that darkness blinds the sightless, that a lit torch or the Light cantrip carves it back, that attacks into the dark roll with Disadvantage (and unseen attackers gain Advantage), that targeted spells refuse a target you can't see, and that the undead in the Blackthroat see you long before you see them.
customIntroduction: Afternoon light lies easy over the verge — grass, the smell of warm stone, a kestrel somewhere overhead. Ahead the ground tilts down into a wound in the hillside: a ragged rock mouth the locals call the Blackthroat, and past its lip the daylight simply stops, as if the dark inside were a held breath. Something in there has been waiting longer than you've been alive, and it can see the doorway you're standing in far better than you can see it.
customContext:
DEMO — LIGHT & VISION (US-127). This room teaches the SRD light rules. THE GEOGRAPHY: the western half is 'the Sunlit Verge' (bright grassland, no obscurance) and the eastern ~70% is 'the Blackthroat' (a carved cave region baked DARK — heavily obscured to anyone without darkvision or carried light, entered through a single rock mouth). Two skeletons and a zombie (faction undead) hold the cave's depths; they have Darkvision and will see the player approaching the mouth long before the player can see them.

SETUP — on encounter start, call add_item with item_id "torch" (reason: "a pitch-soaked brand from the verge") so the player has a light source to test, and tell them plainly in-fiction that they have it and can LIGHT it. (If the player is a caster who knows the Light cantrip, mention that too.)

WHAT TO DEMONSTRATE — narrate these as they arise; the engine resolves them automatically, do NOT fake the dice:
1. Walking past the rock mouth without light: the player is effectively blinded in the dark. Attacks they make into the Blackthroat roll with Disadvantage (swinging at a location), and the undead — who see them — attack with Advantage as unseen attackers.
2. Targeted spells (a wizard's Fire Bolt / Ray of Frost) REFUSE a target the caster can't see in the dark — the cast is wasted with a 'beyond sight' message. The fix is light, not more spells.
3. LIGHT the torch (LIGHT button — a free object interaction): a 20-foot bright radius travels with the player, lifting the dark to bright/dim around them, restoring mutual sight so attacks and spells resolve normally. DOUSE plunges it back.
4. The Light cantrip does the same hands-free for a caster.

The undead never leave the cave's dark willingly (it's their advantage). Let the player choose: fight blind, bait the undead into the sunlit verge where the player can see fine, or light up and push in. There is no objective beyond the lesson — clearing the undead completes it.
objective: Push into the Blackthroat. Learn what darkness does to a fight — and what a torch does to darkness.

then.message: DEMO: the west is bright grassland; the east is the DARK Blackthroat cave. Without darkvision you can't see into the dark — attacks in there roll Disadvantage, the undead (who see you) get Advantage, and targeted spells refuse an unseen target. You start with a TORCH: press LIGHT to carry a 20 ft bright radius and even the odds. Try fighting blind, baiting the undead into the sun, and lighting up.
